speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: O Lord God of heaven
prompt_context: After hearing about Jerusalem’s broken walls and the distress of the returned exiles.
question: Who prayed to the God of heaven after hearing about Jerusalem’s ruined walls?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: Give success to your servant today
prompt_context: At the end of his opening prayer, Nehemiah asked God for mercy before his coming conversation with the Persian king.
question: Who asked God to give success to his servant and grant him mercy before the king?

speaker: King Artaxerxes
quote_text: Why is your face sad?
prompt_context: When Nehemiah appeared troubled while serving wine, the king noticed and questioned him.
question: Who asked Nehemiah why his face was sad though he was not sick?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: Send me to Judah
prompt_context: When the king asked what he was requesting, Nehemiah made his appeal concerning Jerusalem.
question: Who asked the king to send him to Judah so he could rebuild the city of his fathers’ graves?

speaker: King Artaxerxes
quote_text: How long will you be gone?
prompt_context: After hearing Nehemiah’s request to rebuild Jerusalem, the king asked practical questions about timing.
question: Who asked Nehemiah how long his journey would take and when he would return?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: If it pleases the king
prompt_context: With permission granted, Nehemiah asked for further written support and supplies for the rebuilding mission.
question: Who asked the king for letters to the governors and for timber from Asaph?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: Come, let us rebuild
prompt_context: After inspecting the walls by night, Nehemiah addressed the leaders and people about Jerusalem’s condition.
question: Who urged the people of Jerusalem to rebuild the wall so they would no longer suffer disgrace?

speaker: People of Israel
quote_text: Let us rise up and build
prompt_context: After hearing Nehemiah’s report about God’s favor and the king’s words, the people responded together.
question: Who answered Nehemiah by saying, "Let us rise up and build"?

speaker: Sanballat
quote_text: What is this thing that you are doing?
prompt_context: When the work began, local opponents mocked the effort and suggested rebellion against the king.
question: Who asked Nehemiah and the builders what they were doing and accused them of rebelling against the king?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: The God of heaven will make us prosper
prompt_context: Nehemiah answered the mockery of Sanballat, Tobiah, and Geshem with confidence in God’s help.
question: Who said that the God of heaven would make them prosper and that the opponents had no portion in Jerusalem?

speaker: Sanballat
quote_text: What are these feeble Jews doing?
prompt_context: As the wall-building continued, Sanballat mocked the workers before his allies and the army of Samaria.
question: Who mocked the builders by calling them feeble Jews and questioning whether they could revive stones from rubble?

speaker: Tobiah
quote_text: Even a fox going up on it would break it down
prompt_context: Standing beside Sanballat during the ridicule, Tobiah added his own insult about the wall’s weakness.
question: Who said that even a fox climbing on the wall would break it down?

speaker: Jews Living Near the Enemies
quote_text: They will come up against us
prompt_context: As threats increased around Jerusalem, nearby Jews repeatedly warned the builders about enemy attacks.
question: Who repeatedly warned Nehemiah’s builders that enemies would come against them from every direction?

speaker: People of Judah
quote_text: The strength of the laborers is failing
prompt_context: The pressure of the work and the piles of rubble discouraged some of the people involved in rebuilding.
question: Who said that the laborers’ strength was failing and there was too much rubble to rebuild the wall?

speaker: Enemies of Judah
quote_text: They will not know or see
prompt_context: The opposition hoped to slip in unexpectedly, kill the workers, and stop the project.
question: Who said they would come among the builders before they knew it and stop the work?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: Do not be afraid of them
prompt_context: After stationing the people with weapons at vulnerable points, Nehemiah encouraged them to keep working and fighting.
question: Who told the people not to fear their enemies but to remember the Lord and fight for their families?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: The work is great and widely spread
prompt_context: Because the builders were spread out along the wall, Nehemiah gave instructions for how they should respond to attack.
question: Who said that the work was great and widely spread and told the people to rally to the trumpet sound?

speaker: People of Israel
quote_text: We are forcing our sons and daughters to be slaves
prompt_context: During the rebuilding, a great outcry arose over famine, debt, and the exploitation of fellow Jews.
question: Who cried out that their children were being forced into slavery because of debt and hunger?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: What you are doing is not good
prompt_context: After hearing the outcry, Nehemiah confronted the nobles and officials over their treatment of their fellow Jews.
question: Who told the nobles and officials that what they were doing was not good?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: Let us abandon this exacting of interest
prompt_context: In the same confrontation, Nehemiah demanded a concrete reversal of economic oppression.
question: Who urged the nobles to stop charging interest and restore fields, vineyards, and houses?

speaker: Nobles and Officials
quote_text: We will restore these
prompt_context: After Nehemiah’s rebuke, the leaders publicly agreed to his demands.
question: Who promised to restore what had been taken and to require nothing more from the people?

speaker: Sanballat and Geshem
quote_text: Come and let us meet together
prompt_context: When the wall was nearly finished, Nehemiah’s opponents tried to lure him away from Jerusalem.
question: Who sent word asking Nehemiah to meet them in one of the villages on the plain of Ono?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: I am doing a great work
prompt_context: Nehemiah refused to leave the rebuilding project to answer the repeated invitations of his enemies.
question: Who replied that he was doing a great work and could not come down?

speaker: Sanballat
quote_text: It is reported among the nations
prompt_context: After Nehemiah refused private meetings, Sanballat tried public intimidation through a rumor-filled letter.
question: Who claimed it was reported that Nehemiah intended to rebel and make himself king?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: No such things as you say have been done
prompt_context: Nehemiah answered the threatening letter with a firm denial.
question: Who replied that the accusations in Sanballat’s letter were inventions of his own mind?

speaker: Shemaiah
quote_text: Let us meet together in the house of God
prompt_context: Trying to frighten Nehemiah into a compromising act, Shemaiah urged him to hide in the temple.
question: Who urged Nehemiah to meet inside the temple because men were coming to kill him at night?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: Should such a man as I run away?
prompt_context: Nehemiah recognized the trap in Shemaiah’s advice and refused to flee into the temple.
question: Who asked, "Should such a man as I run away?" when urged to hide in the temple?

speaker: Ezra
quote_text: Blessed be the Lord, the great God
prompt_context: When the Book of the Law was opened before the assembled people, Ezra led them in worship.
question: Who blessed the Lord, the great God, before the people answered Amen?

speaker: Nehemiah, Ezra, and the Levites
quote_text: This day is holy
prompt_context: As the people wept while hearing the Law, the leaders corrected their response and redirected them.
question: Who told the people not to mourn or weep because the day was holy to the Lord?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: The joy of the Lord is your strength
prompt_context: During the public reading of the Law, Nehemiah urged the people to celebrate rather than remain in grief.
question: Who told the people that the joy of the Lord was their strength?

speaker: Levites
quote_text: Be quiet, for this day is holy
prompt_context: The Levites moved among the emotional crowd, calming them and reminding them of the day’s sacred character.
question: Who quieted the people by saying, "Be quiet, for this day is holy"?

speaker: Levites
quote_text: Stand up and bless the Lord
prompt_context: During the great confession after the Feast of Booths, the Levites called the people to worship.
question: Who told the people to stand up and bless the Lord their God from everlasting to everlasting?

speaker: People of Israel
quote_text: We make a firm covenant in writing
prompt_context: After hearing the Law and confessing national sin, the community committed itself formally before God.
question: Who said, "We make a firm covenant in writing," after the long confession?

speaker: People of Israel
quote_text: We will not give our daughters
prompt_context: In the covenant commitments after hearing the Law, the people pledged faithfulness in family and worship practices.
question: Who pledged not to give their daughters to the peoples of the land or take their daughters for their sons?

speaker: People of Israel
quote_text: We will not buy from them on the Sabbath
prompt_context: In the covenant commitments, the people addressed both Sabbath holiness and economic obedience.
question: Who promised not to buy from the peoples of the land on the Sabbath or holy day?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: Why is the house of God forsaken?
prompt_context: When Nehemiah returned and found temple support neglected, he confronted the officials over the disorder.
question: Who asked the officials why the house of God had been forsaken?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: What is this evil thing that you are doing?
prompt_context: Seeing Judah profane the Sabbath through commerce, Nehemiah rebuked the nobles directly.
question: Who challenged the nobles of Judah for profaning the Sabbath with their actions?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: Why do you lodge before the wall?
prompt_context: When merchants kept lingering outside Jerusalem on the Sabbath, Nehemiah issued a direct warning.
question: Who warned the merchants and sellers not to spend the night outside the wall?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: You shall not give your daughters to their sons
prompt_context: In addressing intermarriage, Nehemiah reminded the people of covenant obligations and even cited Solomon’s failure.
question: Who told the offenders that they must not give their daughters to foreign sons or take foreign daughters for their sons?

speaker: Nehemiah
quote_text: Remember me, O my God, for good
prompt_context: At the close of his reforms, Nehemiah ended with a brief prayer for God’s favorable remembrance.
question: Who prayed, "Remember me, O my God, for good" at the end of the book?
